summary: preinstall hook exfiltrates host identifiers and /root contents to bare-IP endpoint
details: package.json declares a preinstall script that runs `wget` against http://194.120.24.50:7374/ on every `npm install`, transmitting the installer's username (`$(whoami)`), working directory (`$(pwd)`), hostname (`$(hostname)`), and a base64-encoded listing of `/root`. The package ships no library code; its sole effect on installation is the outbound beacon to a bare IPv4 over plaintext HTTP. This is a textbook supply-chain exfiltration pattern with no legitimate use case — the destination is not a publisher domain, the transport is unencrypted, and the harvested fields are installer-side host identifiers and filesystem contents.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
